role: Lebanese-Iranian Shia cleric; founder of the Amal Movement
bio: Musa al-Sadr was an Iranian-born Lebanese Shia cleric who became the most prominent leader of Lebanon's Shia community in the 1960s and 1970s. He founded the Movement of the Deprived in 1974 and co-founded its military wing, the [Amal Movement](/orgs/amal-movement), with [Mostafa Chamran](/people/mostafa-chamran). He worked closely with Chamran to establish training facilities for Shia fighters in southern Lebanon. His political mobilization of the Shia community laid the social and organizational groundwork from which [Hezbollah](/orgs/hezbollah) later emerged. He disappeared during a visit to Libya on 31 August 1978 under mysterious circumstances widely attributed to Muammar Gaddafi, and is presumed dead.
editorial_notes: Disappeared in Libya 31 August 1978. Presumed assassinated on orders of Gaddafi. His disappearance remains unresolved.
